title: Form style declared explicitly per form purpose on the settings sheet
description: OC4 supports four form styles. The recent OC4 default flipped to 'Simple-Single Page' per release notes. Each form's `style` column on the settings sheet should be populated deliberately, never left blank by default. DEFAULTS BY FORM PURPOSE: site-staff form with <=30 items → (blank) Simple-single (default; clean for short forms); site-staff form with dense tabular data (LB, VS panels) → theme-grid (grid layout suits tabular data); site-staff form with >50 items → pages (pagination helps cognitive load); Participate/ePRO form → pages (OC blog: 'fewer questions on more pages is preferable' + portrait-phone optimization). Forms sourced from customer_oc4_standard or customer_crf_library are used as-authored.
rationale: Explicit style assignment makes form rendering predictable and matches each form's content shape to the optimal layout. Dense tabular forms (vital signs, lab panels) read better in a grid; long forms benefit from pagination to reduce cognitive load; short site-staff forms are fine as a single page. ePRO forms on mobile particularly benefit from pagination since portrait-phone screens force long forms into excessive scrolling.

flag.message: Form ${form.form_id} (definition_source=cdash_default) — set settings.style explicitly. Defaults: site-staff <=30 items → blank (Simple-single); site-staff dense tabular (LB, VS) → theme-grid; site-staff >50 items → pages; Participate/ePRO → pages. Customer-sourced forms exempt.

effect.soft: Determine form purpose: is_participate / is_epro flag, or item count (excluding note/calculate/group-marker rows). Apply style per the defaults: blank for short site forms; theme-grid for dense tabular site forms; pages for long site forms (>50 items) or any Participate/ePRO form. Surface counts in conventions_applied.form_style_explicit.{site_simple_single, site_simple_pages, site_theme_grid, participate_simple_pages, missing_style}.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §14 during Phase B.1d. Hybrid: the form-purpose detection (Participate/ePRO vs site-staff, item-count thresholds) requires resolving spec attributes plus survey row counting which isn't a clean DSL primitive yet. Delegated to soft directive. A future DSL extension could surface form.purpose and form.data_row_count as resolvable attributes, promoting this to structured.
